title: Opening the bank app and seeing seven figures
subtitle: A short guided visualisation for making a seven-figure balance feel ordinary rather than impossible.
excerpt: A visualisation practice from Day 24 of the MONEY program. Use to rehearse the moment of opening your banking app and seeing a balance you once thought impossible.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Feel worthy of millions."

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 24 of the MONEY program. The premise is that the nervous system doesn't distinguish between a rehearsed scene and a real one as clearly as we think. Running the scene repeatedly (phone in hand, number on screen, body staying calm) trains the response you want before the event arrives.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes. Take two slow breaths, in through the nose, out through the mouth.
Picture yourself holding your phone. The screen is on. You open your banking app, the usual tap, the usual fingerprint or code. The app loads.
Look at the balance. The number begins with a one or a two or a five, followed by six more digits. Seven figures. Watch it sit there on the screen, ordinary as a grocery bill.
Notice your body's first reaction. If there is a flicker of disbelief, let it pass without argument. You're not trying to convince yourself. You're just watching the number.
Stay with the screen. Your shoulders are soft. Your jaw is loose. This number is yours. The money is yours. The account has your name on it.
Put the phone down on the table beside you, face up, number visible. Walk away from it. Come back. Look again. Same number.
When you are ready, open your eyes. The scene is complete.
Adapted from Day 24 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
